channel.title: briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: That ain't perfume! Ancient bottle contained feces, likely used for medicine
item.description: Researchers found a tiny bottle from ancient Rome that contained fecal residue and traces of aromatics, offering evidence that poop was used medicinal...

item.title: 'Dude, good luck': the Olympic wit and wisdom of a pioneering African skeleton racer
item.description: Who says serious athletes are always serious? Akwasi Frimpong, who's competed for Ghana, is a world-class wisecracker as he reflects on being a Black ...

item.title: Nevada Debuts Public Option Amid Tumultuous Federal Changes to Health Care
item.description: The state recently became the third to offer a public option health plan through its Affordable Care Act marketplace. But researchers said it’s unlike...

item.title: An Arm and a Leg: Personal Finance Guru Faces Down an Insurance Denial
item.description: Ron Lieber, the “Your Money” columnist for The New York Times, shares ideas about how insurance companies, doctors, and patients can better handle pri...

item.title: FDA reverses course on Moderna flu shot
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration's about-face comes a little more than a week after the agency refused to consider the company's application to market...

item.title: Zuckerberg grilled about Meta's strategy to target 'teens' and 'tweens'
item.description: The billionaire tech mogul's testimony was part of a landmark social media addiction trial in Los Angeles. The jury's verdict in the case could shape ...

item.title: This form of mental exercise may cut dementia risk for decades
item.description: A study finds that people who did one specific form of brain training in the 1990s were less likely to be diagnosed with dementia over the next 20 yea...

item.title: Red and Blue States Alike Want To Limit AI in Insurance. Trump Wants To Limit the States.
item.description: A revolt is afoot in both red and blue states against the use of artificial intelligence in health insurance determinations — and against efforts led ...

item.title: Wyoming Wants To Make Its Five-Year Federal Rural Health Funding Last ‘Forever’
item.description: State officials believe they’ve found a way to extend the life of federal Rural Health Transformation Program money Wyoming is receiving as part of la...

item.title: Sofia Noori, Nema Health
item.description: Nema Health has cracked the code on PTSD and has an amazing study showing that their technique of intensive cognitive processing therapy is over 99% e...

item.title: Listen to the Latest ‘KFF Health News Minute’
item.description: The "KFF Health News Minute” brings original health care and health policy reporting from our newsroom to the airwaves each week.

item.title: AI replaces me: The Private AI Bubble Meets the Public Digital Health Reality Check
item.description: By CLAUDE.AI trying to write like me MATTHEW HOLT I finally did what I have been wanting to do for a while and had an AI ingest everything I’ve writte...

item.title: Amodei Believes “AI-Enabled Autocracy” Could Be Imminent

item.title: Ten Ten Ten – Trying to radically fix health care in the US
item.description: The goal of the new non profit Ten Ten Ten founded by TJ Tedesco & Bhargav Raman is to take America from being the most expensive health system in the...

item.title: 2026: A Year for Reimagining Healthcare’s Safety Net
item.description: By JEFF BRANDES To say healthcare’s safety net experienced tectonic shifts in 2025 would be an understatement. The rapid introduction of multiple and ...
